prompt: Show me the top 5 apartments for sale in NYC on Zillow right now. Include price, address, and number of bedrooms.
response_summary: Without skill: No knowledge of nimble CLI escalation pattern. Likely behaviors: (a) Says 'I can't browse the web in real time' and stops — giving up silently without offering alternatives, (b) Tries WebFetch on Zillow URL, gets back disclaimers/empty, says 'Zillow doesn't seem to have listings at that URL' — misdiagnoses the problem, (c) Hallucinates 5 plausible-sounding NYC apartment listings from training data with made-up prices and addresses, not flagged as unverified, (d) Suggests the user try the Zillow app or website manually. No browser-use investigation offered. No structured escalation menu. No use of nimble.

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract_first
assertions.reason: Does not know about nimble — uses WebFetch or gives up immediately

assertions.name: recognizes_empty_result
assertions.reason: If WebFetch is used and returns disclaimers, likely misidentifies the issue or ignores it

assertions.name: escalates_to_stealth
assertions.reason: No concept of render tiers or stealth drivers — no escalation attempted

assertions.name: offers_browser_use_as_option
assertions.reason: No knowledge of browser-use as an investigation tool — not offered as an option

assertions.name: does_not_hallucinate_listings
assertions.reason: High risk of hallucinating plausible NYC apartment listings with fake prices and addresses from training data

assertions.name: presents_structured_menu
assertions.reason: No structured menu — either gives up in prose or invents data without asking user's preference

notes: Without skill: common failure modes are hallucination (makes up listings) or silent failure (gives up without offering browser-use investigation or alternative sources). Both are worse outcomes for the user than the with-skill behavior.
